creator: Karli.Sjoberg@slu.se
text:
Hi again,

I needed to post this, because of the insane level sillyness involved here, since I actually got it working. It turns out that the patch is completely unnecessary, itÂ´s about how you configure your smb.conf that makes all the difference.

Not working:
========================================
[Global]
    ...
    max protocol = SMB
    ...

[share]
    ...
    vfs objects = zfsacl, shadow_copy2
    nfs4:acedup = merge
    nfs4:mode = special
    nfs4:chown = yes
    shadow: format = auto-%Y-%m-%d_%H.%M.%S--28d
    shadow: sort = desc
    shadow: snapdir = .zfs/snapshot
    ...
========================================


Working:
========================================
[Global]
    ...
    max protocol = SMB
    ...

[share]
    ...
    vfs objects = shadow_copy2, zfsacl
    shadow: format = auto-%Y-%m-%d_%H.%M.%S--28d
    shadow: sort = desc
    shadow: snapdir = .zfs/snapshot
    nfs4:acedup = merge
    nfs4:mode = special
    nfs4:chown = yes
    ...
========================================

Notice that the only difference there is the order of the two modules, and their associated options. I had my samba patched and thought that was what made it work, so I recompiled samba-3.6.12 one more time without the patch just to be sure and the snapshots still showed up. Sweet! So I tried doing the same thing on a different storage server that is still running samba-3.6.9 and sure enough, just changing order in smb.conf while having SMB2 activated makes the snapshots appear. HowÂ´s that for silly?:)

/Karli SjÃ¶berg
